name: Christmas Candy Canes
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup crushed peppermint candy canes (or hard peppermint candies)
1/2 cup (1 stick) salted butter, at room temperature
recipeInstructions:
1. Adjust two oven racks to divide the oven into thirds. Preheat the oven to 375 degrees F (190 C). Have ready two ungreased baking sheets.
2. In a small bowl, mix the granulated sugar with the crushed candy canes; set aside.
3. In a large bowl, beat together the butter, shortening, confectioners' sugar, egg, vanilla extract, and peppermint extract until light and fluffy, about 2 or 3 minutes.
4. With the mixer on medium-low speed, gradually add the flour, beating just until blended.
5. Remove half of the dough from the bowl and set aside on a sheet of waxed paper. To the remaining dough, add the red food coloring and beat until evenly colored.
6. (The dough can be tightly wrapped separately in aluminum foil and refrigerated for up to a week or frozen for up to three months. If frozen, thaw in the refrigerator and bring to room temperature before proceeding.)
7. For each candy cane, scoop 1 teaspoonful of the plain dough and an equal amount of pink dough. Roll each scoop between the palms of your hands to form a 4-inch rope.
8. Twist the ropes together and shape into a candy cane. As they are made, arrange the canes on an ungreased baking sheet, spacing them about 1 inch apart.
9. Bake for about 9 minutes until firm to the touch and just golden. Reverse the baking sheets on the racks and from front to back once during baking.
10. Immediately sprinkle each cookie with the sugar-and-peppermint mixture using a wide turner. Transfer the cookies to wire racks to cool completely.
11. Store in a tightly covered container, separating the layers with sheets of waxed paper.
Yield: About 48 cookies
